title: Severe epigastric pain radiating to the back in a 48-year-old
presentation: A 48-year-old male with a history of heavy alcohol use presents with 12 hours of severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, partially relieved by leaning forward, with nausea and vomiting. Vitals: BP 108/72, HR 118, RR 20, Temp 38.3C, SpO2 97%. Exam: epigastric tenderness with guarding, mild distension, decreased bowel sounds, mild jaundice, Grey Turner sign on flanks.

urgent: CT abdomen with contrast at 48-72h if not improving
gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Lipase >3x upper limit = pancreatitis. BISAP or Ranson's criteria for severity. Aggressive fluid resuscitation is cornerstone of treatment. Grey Turner sign = severe hemorrhagic pancreatitis. CT only if diagnosis unclear or not improving at 48-72h.
